# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

FROM debian:bullseye-slim

# Update package lists
RUN apt-get update

# Install essential tools for adding new repositories and secure apt, and curl for fetching
RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    gnupg

# Add Node.js 20.x (LTS) repository from NodeSource
# Instructions adapted from https://github.com/nodesource/distributions/blob/master/README.md
RUN mkdir -p /etc/apt/keyrings
RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/gpgkey/nodesource-repo.gpg.key | gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/nodesource.gpg
RUN echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/nodesource.gpg] https://deb.nodesource.com/node_20.x nodistro main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/nodesource.list

# Update apt-get again to recognize the new Node.js repository
RUN apt-get update

# Install Node.js (which includes npm)
RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends nodejs

# Install build tools and other common system utilities
# build-essential includes gcc, g++, make, etc., often required for native Node.js modules
RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    git \
    python3 \
    wget

# Clean up apt caches to reduce image size
RUN apt-get clean
RUN rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set the working directory for the application
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json first to leverage Docker cache
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./

# Install application dependencies
# npm ci is recommended for clean, reproducible builds in CI/CD and production environments
RUN npm ci

# Copy the rest of the application code
COPY . .

# If your application has a build step (e.g., for frontend assets or transpilation)
# RUN npm run build

# Expose the port your Node.js application listens on
EXPOSE 3000

# Command to run the application
# Replace 'src/index.js' with your application's actual entry file
CMD ["node", "src/index.js"]
